I can see MDS_FMODE_EXEC is 0x4 while FMODE_EXEC(kernel) is 0x20. That needs to be handled. Checking why this should assert if fmode is 1.

<snip>
LustreError: 13395:0:(file.c:309:ll_md_real_close()) ###### fmode = 1 write=2, exex=4, read=1
LustreError: 13395:0:(file.c:309:ll_md_real_close()) Skipped 209 previous similar messages

Message from syslogd@centos79 at Oct  4 02:58:19 ...
 kernel:LustreError: 8075:0:(file.c:310:ll_md_real_close()) ASSERTION( fd_open_mode & MDS_FMODE_READ ) failed:

Patch Set 1:

> I can see MDS_FMODE_EXEC is 0x4 while FMODE_EXEC(kernel) is 0x20.
 > That needs to be handled. Checking why this should assert if fmode
 > is 1.

That is exactly why we have our own MDS_FMODE_* constants here, because the in-kernel FMODE_* constants have changed several times over the years, which caused problems when clients/servers were not running the same kernel version.  They are only useful internally and not over the network.

commit.message:
LU-12885 mds: add enums for MDS_OPEN flags (3/4)

This patch is third of the series of patch that separates
kernel open flags from MDS open flags

This third step completes all of the MDS-side changes

This patch also changes/adds kernel-style doc instead
of old doxygen style wherever required
